_comment: Cortex Agent REST API payload template for the external incident orchestrator. The orchestrator POSTs this payload to https://<account>.snowflakecomputing.com/api/v2/cortex/agent:run with a valid Bearer token to retrieve incident evidence. Replace placeholder values before use.

tool_spec.name: cortex_analyst_grizl
tool_spec.description: Query structured GRIZL observability telemetry: HTTP request error rates, latency distributions, error signatures, deployment history, and forwarder health. Use for questions with deterministic SQL answers over time-series data.

tool_spec.name: cortex_search_grizl_knowledge
tool_spec.description: Semantic search over GRIZL postmortems, runbooks, and architecture notes. Use for historical context, similar past incidents, and recommended remediation steps.

content.text: What are the most recent 5xx errors for grizl-backend on route /api/chat in the last 30 minutes? Include deployment SHA and error signature.

_incident_orchestrator_notes.auth: Generate a short-lived JWT or OAuth token per request. For M2M: use Snowflake service account key-pair auth. See: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/sql-api/authenticating
_incident_orchestrator_notes.evidence_question_template: What are the most recent errors for {service} on route {route} with deployment SHA {deployment_sha} in the last 30 minutes? Include error signature, error rate, and any related anomaly signals.
_incident_orchestrator_notes.response_format: The agent returns a streaming SSE response. Parse 'data:' lines as JSON, accumulate 'delta' content blocks of type 'text', join into the evidence summary string.
_incident_orchestrator_notes.rate_limits: Cortex Agent requests count against your Snowflake Cortex token budget. Budget is per-account and per-model. Monitor usage in Snowflake account usage views: SNOWFLAKE.ACCOUNT_USAGE.CORTEX_ANALYST_USAGE_HISTORY.
_incident_orchestrator_notes.fallback: If the Cortex Agent API is unavailable, fall back to direct SQL: SELECT ... FROM GRIZL.OBSERVABILITY.APPLICATION_ERRORS WHERE SERVICE = '{service}' AND INGEST_TIMESTAMP >= DATEADD('minute', -30, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP()) LIMIT 10
